Title: Zero-touch always-on stack
Requirement: The stack shall bring every service up unattended from an autoinstall image and self-heal on reboot/crash.
Rationale: Always-on infra must not need manual clicks (SN-001).
AcceptanceCriteria: autoinstall+first-boot start all services with no prompts; every service has restart:unless-stopped and a healthcheck; power-cycle returns all services within ~1 min on a Docker host.

Title: Split-horizon DNS + edge TLS + blocklists
Requirement: Technitium shall serve split-horizon A records to LAN_IP and Caddy shall obtain a public cert valid on-LAN, with ad-blocklists enabled.
Rationale: One box owns LAN name resolution and edge TLS (SN-002).
AcceptanceCriteria: provision-technitium.sh (idempotent) creates the zone, records, forwarders, and blocklists; dig against the box returns LAN_IP; https on-LAN shows no cert warning.

Title: Google-auth gate for the tracker
Requirement: The tracker subdomain shall be fronted by oauth2-proxy (Google provider) that admits only allow-listed accounts and forwards X-Forwarded-Email/-User to the tracker.
Rationale: Multi-user v1 needs real identity without a bespoke auth system (D1/D2/D3).
AcceptanceCriteria: oauth2-proxy config sets provider=google, an authenticated-emails allow-list, PASS_USER_HEADERS true, upstream=tracker:8787; a non-allow-listed account is rejected; verified on a Docker host.

Title: Tracker never directly reachable
Requirement: The tracker container shall be bridge-only (expose, not host-published) so its only ingress is via oauth2-proxy.
Rationale: Trusted headers are forgeable if the port is open (D2/WI-10.5).
AcceptanceCriteria: docker-compose.yml uses expose:8787 with no ports: mapping for tracker; Caddy routes the host to oauth2-proxy only.

Title: basic_auth retained for single-user services
Requirement: Caddy shall apply basic_auth to the actual and dns hosts and NOT to the tracker host.
Rationale: Only the multi-user tracker needs OAuth; keep the simple gate elsewhere (D2).
AcceptanceCriteria: Caddyfile imports protect_actual/protect_dns on those hosts; the tracker host has no basic_auth directive.

Title: Tracker image consumed as naglight:local
Requirement: Compose shall reference image naglight:local with a documented local build step and a commented build-from-sibling fallback; the migrated Dockerfile is reference-only. Dev boxes resolve locally-built images via a permissive chain: already present, else built from a sibling checkout, else pulled from a DECLARED public image knob (empty until the private app repo publishes), else a loud failure naming all three fixes.
Rationale: Q10.2 build-locally, no registry yet; NagLight owns the canonical build (D1/WI-10.4); NagLight/Finance-Auditor are PRIVATE, so a checkout must not be the only path once a public image exists.
AcceptanceCriteria: tracker service uses image naglight:${TRACKER_IMAGE_TAG}; README documents 'docker build -t naglight:local ../NagLight'; a commented build: fallback points at ../../NagLight; tracker/*.deprecated are clearly marked; scripts/ensure-local-images.sh implements the present->sibling->TRACKER_PUBLIC_IMAGE->fail chain and run-sim.sh invokes it.

Title: SSH key-only + Cockpit + unattended-upgrades
Requirement: Autoinstall shall enable SSH with key-only auth, install Cockpit reachable LAN-only, and enable unattended OS security upgrades.
Rationale: The headless box must be manageable over the LAN without physical visits (SN-005, KEY PIECE).
AcceptanceCriteria: user-data sets allow-pw:false with authorized-keys and a locked password; installs+enables cockpit.socket and unattended-upgrades; Cockpit is not proxied to the public internet.

Title: Remote ops workflow + reimage ladder memo
Requirement: The repo shall document the remote docker-compose workflow and deliver a reimage-over-LAN decision memo with checkboxes; nothing destructive is implemented without Peter's ratification.
Rationale: Trivial remote debugging is a KEY PIECE; reimage is the HIGH-RISK line (only implement past a checkbox).
AcceptanceCriteria: REMOTE_MANAGEMENT.md covers SSH/compose/Cockpit ops and the reimage ladder (GRUB-reinstall / PXE-netboot / smart-plug+USB) with a recommendation and unchecked boxes; no destructive path is wired.

Title: Auxiliary LAN-only observability
Requirement: The stack shall include Uptime-Kuma and Dozzle (and optional self-hosted ntfy), LAN-only, health-checked, every knob in .env.example.
Rationale: Cheap operability for a self-hosted box (SN-006).
AcceptanceCriteria: compose blocks for uptime-kuma and dozzle (ntfy behind a profile) are bridge/LAN-only with healthchecks; every ${VAR} they use has an .env.example key.

Title: Public-facing, secret-free repo
Requirement: The repo shall track only *.example templates and gitignore all filled secrets/state; the local commit identity shall be diytechy, never Peter's name/email.
AcceptanceCriteria: git config user.name=diytechy; .env/.token/allow-list/marker/user-data.filled gitignored; no real secret/hash/email/LAN-detail/personal-name in any tracked file.

Title: Honest config-level validation
Requirement: The check harness shall validate config coverage (env/Caddy/file + YAML parse) and the repo shall record runtime bring-up as PENDING a Docker host.
Rationale: No Docker on the dev box; overclaiming runtime validation is a false green (SN-008).
AcceptanceCriteria: scripts/validate_config.py passes: every ${VAR} in compose has an .env.example key, every Caddy {$VAR} is passed by the caddy service, referenced files exist, YAML parses; docs/status.md records what was NOT exercised.

Title: Opt-in tier-2 service catalog
Requirement: The stack shall define an opt-in catalog of self-hosted services (photos, video, music, podcasts, home automation, passwords, file sync, RSS/recipes/dashboard, update notifications) behind compose profiles — off by default, LAN_IP-bound or bridge-only, every knob in .env.example, images excluded from the baked ISO payload unless their profile is enabled at export.
Rationale: Use the box's headroom without endangering the validated core or bloating the ISO (SN-009); pins-never-drift needs an update NOTIFIER (diun), not an auto-updater.
AcceptanceCriteria: docker compose config lists no tier-2 service unless its profile is enabled; every tier-2 ${VAR} has an .env.example key; tier-2 ports bind LAN_IP or are bridge-only (Vaultwarden deliberately Caddy-only); export-images.sh without EXTRA_PROFILES bakes only the core+ntfy set; public exposure requires the three-step opt-in (profile + Caddyfile site + EXTRA_SUBDOMAINS record).

Title: Docker-volume sources in the backup table
Requirement: backup.sh shall accept volume:VOL[@CONTAINER] and path:/dir source specs in the single BACKUP_SOURCES table alongside //host/share, acquiring volume contents from the volume's mountpoint (docker volume inspect), optionally quiescing @CONTAINER (stop before copy, restart immediately after, EXIT-trap restart on any failure path), with every set then flowing unchanged through archive/hash/manifest/retention/offsite/report.
Rationale: One table drives one execution sequence (Peter 2026-07-10); OI-8: the box's own volumes were backed up by nothing; a live DB copy needs an optional quiesce.
AcceptanceCriteria: Sim (mock-docker call contract + real pipeline): (a) a volume: set archives+hashes+restores byte-equal with zero stop/start calls; (b) volume:VOL@C issues stop before copy and start after, exactly once each, green run; (c) a forced mid-copy failure still restarts the container (EXIT trap) AND posts ok=false nonzero; (d) a cifs-only config makes zero docker calls; comment lines in the table are skipped.

Title: Host the Finance-Auditor pipeline
Requirement: The stack shall define a finance-auditor compose service per FA's IF-003 spec — profile-gated until FA's G-Final then promoted to core — with finance_snapshots + finance_actual_data volumes, FINANCE_* env knobs in .env.example, bridge-internal reachability to actual:5006 and tracker:8787, TZ-honored RUN_AT, and the image resolved via the SR-006 chain with --build-arg ACTUAL_API_VERSION pinned to ACTUAL_IMAGE_TAG; the snapshots volume shall be documented for LOCAL-only backup (never OFFSITE_SETS).
Rationale: FA is G3-complete and ships the image spec (its Dockerfile + DEC-19); the IF-002 pin coupling and the §3 privacy firewall are hosting-side obligations (SN-011).
AcceptanceCriteria: docker compose config lists finance-auditor only with its profile; every FINANCE_* ${VAR} has an .env.example key; ensure-local-images.sh builds finance-auditor:local with the ACTUAL_API_VERSION build-arg; backup.env.example carries the finance volume line with the never-offsite warning; no ports/healthcheck on the service (FA's documented design).
